_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Fetal growth disorders: Growth restriction and Macrosomia
Hypertensive disorders: Chronic Hypertension with superimposed preeclampsia
Diabetes in pregnancy: Pregestational Diabetes Mellitus (DM) – Type 1 & 2
Hematological disorders: Sickle cell disease in pregnancy
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Acute fatty liver of pregnancy
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Hepatic diseases that occur in pregnancy
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Preexisting chronic disease in pregnancy
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Gallstones in pregnancy
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Constipation and Diarrhea
Hepatobiliary and gastrointestinal disorders: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD)
Endocrine disorders and obesity: Thyroid disorders in pregnancy (hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, thyroid storm)
Endocrine disorders and obesity: Adrenal disorders and pregnancy
Endocrine disorders and obesity: Obesity in pregnancy
Respiratory, dermatological, and connective tissue disorders: Asthma
Respiratory, dermatological, and connective tissue disorders: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) in pregnancy
Urinary tract and renal disorders: Urinary tract infections
Urinary tract and renal disorders: Acute renal failure in pregnancy
Urinary tract and renal disorders: Chronic renal diseases in pregnancy
Benign and Malignant Tumors of the Reproductive tract

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, planned line for management, and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history especially current obstetric history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, and planned line of treatment, advice for admission?
Did clinical condition, evaluation findings, and/or imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor Case Papers with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available (if applicable)?
Was the history, clinical findings, and investigations/imaging indicative of admission?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Pregnancy not confirmed by investigations and/or imaging.
Diagnosis or patient's condition not documented as requiring admission for further management.
Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking detailed obstetric history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, planned management, or admission advice.
Indoor Case Papers missing or incomplete at claim submission, lacking daily vitals or line of treatment.
Discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre and indoor care areas including blood transfusion arrangements
clinical_key_pointers: History of trauma / lifting heavy weight
Swelling in any part of the body
Incidental findings during routine antenatal examination
referral_criteria: If woman presents with bleeding per vaginum, after P/A & P/S to confirm amount of bleeding & rule out local causes, all such cases to be referred to CHC or higher centre.
If non compliant or Hb < 9g%, parenteral iron sucrose therapy not administered, or if Hb < 7g%, patient to be referred to higher centre.
Refer to higher centre if any discrepancy between fundal height and period of gestation.
High risk pregnancy patients to be delivered at district hospital/medical college and preferably receive antenatal care at these centres.
additional_information.alos_note: Once diagnosis is established the case can be booked in the relevant package or further stay/admission should be decided based on the level of complications of the disease
